title: Xanthoxalis madrensis
taxonomy: Xanthoxalis madrensis is classified in the family Oxalidaceae, commonly known as the wood sorrel family. This family consists of about 800 species of flowering plants. Xanthoxalis madrensis is a member of the genus Xanthoxalis, which is a group of flowering plants with oxalic acid in their leaves. The genus Xanthoxalis is part of the tribe Oxalideae, which is one of the four tribes within the family Oxalidaceae. Xanthoxalis madrensis is native to Mexico and is found in mountainous regions.

intro: Xanthoxalis madrensis, also known as the Madrean yellow-sorrel, is a perennial plant belonging to the Oxalis genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Xanthoxalis madrensis include oxalic acid, anthraquinones, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used to make a herbal supplement in the form of capsules or tablets.
